page: Microsoft Azure DC24ds_v3 is a memory-optimized server instance belonging to the DDCSv3 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 24 dedicated physical cores and 24 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The CPU features a cache configuration of 48 KB L1d, 1 MB L2, and 48 MB L3 cache, and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 (AVX512F and AVX512_VNNI), AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with a 1843 GB local disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 15933.0 and a static web serving throughput of 43.73 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. Image processing performance is also strong at 15819.0 on Geekbench. In contrast, single-core CPU performance is average, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1897.99 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1680.0. Memory bandwidth tests show an average read-only small block cached speed of 2531.59 GB/sec, while uncached large block read and write speeds reach 163.72 GB/sec and 66.28 GB/sec respectively. Large-scale LLM inference reveals a performance tradeoff, demonstrating strong throughput for small 135M models but weak performance for large 70B models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the DC24ds_v3 offers a dense allocation of dedicated physical cores and a substantial memory footprint without the overhead of hyperthreading. This makes it highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, static web serving, and moderate-scale machine learning inference. The inclusion of a large local disk and Premium Storage capability further optimizes it for database deployments and applications requiring fast local scratch space.
description: Microsoft Azure DC24ds_v3 is a memory-optimized server instance from the DDCSv3 family, featuring 24 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, alongside a bundled 1843 GB local disk. Benchmark results show strong multi-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench multi-core score of 15933.0 and a static web serving throughput of 43.73 GB/sec. However, single-core performance and large-model LLM inference represent notable tradeoffs, with text generation on a 70B model dropping to 2.74 tokens/sec. This instance is well-suited for database operations, memory-intensive applications, and static web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC24ds_v3 is a memory-optimized server featuring 24 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 192 GB RAM, and a 1843 GB local disk, delivering strong multi-core and web serving performance.

tagline: A memory-optimized server with dedicated Intel Xeon cores and high-capacity local storage for database and web serving workloads.

192.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core

Strong multi-core performance and static web serving throughput
Suited for database operations, memory-intensive tasks, and web serving
